steps.action: Send /v1/completions with ASCII prompt 'Hello world', logprobs=true, top_logprobs=5
steps.expected: text_offset matches cumulative UTF-8 byte lengths of tokens
steps.observed: All 11 byte offsets match expected cumulative values

steps.action: Send /v1/completions with Japanese prompt 'こんにちは世界'
steps.expected: Multi-byte characters counted correctly in byte offsets
steps.observed: All 11 byte offsets match expected cumulative values (Japanese tokens counted correctly)
All 11 byte offsets match expected cumulative values (emoji counted as 4 bytes each)

steps.action: Send /v1/completions with Latin diacritics 'Café résumé naïve'
steps.observed: All 11 byte offsets match expected cumulative values
All 11 byte offsets match expected cumulative values

assertions.title: top_logprobs: 0 produces valid but minimal dictionaries

steps.expected: Each top_logprobs entry is null (position 0) or dict with 1 key
steps.observed: Position 0 has dict with 1 key (acceptable per contract). All other positions have dict with exactly 1 key (chosen token only)

steps.action: Verify top_logprobs array is not omitted
Verify each non-position-0 dict has exactly 1 key
steps.observed: All 10 non-position-0 entries have exactly 1 key

steps.action: Send 7 diverse requests (ASCII, Japanese, emoji, Latin diacritics, Cyrillic, Korean, English)
steps.expected: text_offset arrays populated and correct in all 7 responses
steps.observed: All 7 requests returned correct text_offset arrays. Multi-byte UTF-8 characters (Japanese 3-byte, emoji 4-byte, Cyrillic 2-byte, Korean 3-byte) all computed correctly

steps.action: Verify text_offset length matches tokens length

assertions.title: Final token_logprobs entry is the last generated token's logprob

steps.action: Send /v1/completions with max_tokens=5, verify token_logprobs length equals tokens length
steps.expected: token_logprobs array length == tokens array length
steps.observed: Length match confirmed (6 tokens, 6 logprobs). finish_reason=length

steps.action: Verify token_logprobs[-1] is a valid float (not null)
steps.expected: Last entry is a float, not null

steps.action: Repeat with max_tokens=3, verify length match and valid last float
steps.expected: token_logprobs length == tokens length, last is float

steps.observed: All 5 requests: length match confirmed, last logprob is valid float. No trailing null appended.

assertions.title: Legacy streaming /v1/completions forwards logprobs in SSE chunks

steps.action: Send streaming /v1/completions with stream=true, logprobs=true, top_logprobs=5
steps.expected: SSE stream contains logprobs data in choices[0]
steps.observed: 22 SSE chunks parsed. 10 chunks contain logprobs data, 11 are text-only deltas, 1 is [DONE]

steps.action: Verify logprobs in chunks use legacy format keys
steps.expected: logprobs objects contain tokens, token_logprobs, top_logprobs, text_offset
steps.observed: All 10 logprobs chunks have all 4 legacy format keys
Sample chunk has top_logprobs with 2 entries showing alternative tokens

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed (VAL-LEG-005, VAL-LEG-006, VAL-LEG-007, VAL-LEG-008, VAL-COMP-009). text_offset byte offsets are correctly computed for multi-byte UTF-8 characters (Japanese, emoji, Cyrillic, Korean, Latin diacritics). top_logprobs: 0 produces valid minimal single-key dictionaries. token_logprobs array length matches tokens length with no trailing null. Streaming SSE correctly forwards legacy logprobs format in chunks.
